name: Patisiran preserved functional capacity in ATTR-CM
summary: APOLLO-B supports TTR-lowering as a functional human signal in an age-associated amyloid cardiomyopathy, while not proving active aggregate removal.
statement: In ATTR cardiac amyloidosis, 12 months of patisiran preserved 6-minute-walk distance and health-status scores compared with placebo.
population_or_model: Patients with hereditary or wild-type ATTR cardiac amyloidosis
quantitative_note: The 6-minute-walk median difference was 14.69 m (95% CI 0.69 to 28.69; P=0.02), and KCCQ-OS least-squares mean difference was 3.7 points (95% CI 0.2 to 7.2; P=0.04); the composite secondary endpoints were not significant.
caveats: Patisiran inhibits hepatic TTR production rather than directly clearing existing deposits.
The trial is disease-specific and does not demonstrate generalized protein aggregate clearance in aging.
Infusion reactions, arthralgia, and muscle spasms were more common with patisiran.
